picks.rationale: Blowout earnings (+346% YoY revenue to $41.5B, EPS $25.11 vs $20.49 est) and record Q4 guidance of ~$50B vs $43.2B consensus, with $100B in contracted customer agreements locking in high margins through 2030; P/E 27.4, strong_buy consensus, +284.91% YTD.

Micron's earnings reinforce NAND tightness; SNDK itself delivered record Q3 revenue of $5.95B (+97% QoQ) and guided Q4 revenue $7.75-8.25B with 79-81% gross margins; stock +748.35% YTD, +46.90% 1M, buy consensus with strong tailwinds.

Key AI interconnect play; joined S&P 500, received Nvidia strategic investment, and Jensen Huang called it the 'next trillion-dollar company'; +215% YTD, strong_buy consensus (an_score 1.45), positive news sentiment 0.21.

Memory AI play with +707.49% YTD and extremely strong 1M momentum (+46.73%); news sentiment is the most positive in the memory group (0.347) citing memory shortages beyond 2027; P/E 91, buy consensus.

AI compute leader with +222.78% YTD, +34.21% 1M momentum, strong news sentiment 0.351, and P/E 32.6 is reasonable vs growth; analyst buy consensus, benefits from AI server demand and memory supply tailwinds from Micron.

Data center connectivity play with strong_buy consensus (an_score 1.32), +87.15% YTD, +20.93% 1M, and +7.50% 1W; benefits from AI cluster scaling and data movement demand; P/E 106 reflects growth premium.

Memory/HDD play with +257.47% YTD, strong news sentiment 0.342, and P/E 97 is elevated but supported by Micron's structural demand view; analysts maintain buy ratings with price target boosts despite recent 1W pullback.

Memory and storage play up +260.09% YTD, P/E 40.5 is attractive relative to memory peers, high dividend yield 9%; buy consensus, but caution on 1W -5.16% and 1D -6.70% pullback; positive news sentiment 0.034.
Nuclear ops play with +19.64% YTD, +13.54% 1M, P/E 20.8 is the cheapest in the nuclear group; dividend yield 66% is attractive; buy consensus, benefits from AI data center power demand.
Nuclear ops play up +5.06% YTD, +7.15% 1M, +1.71% 1W with buy consensus (an_score 1.59); benefits from increasing AI-driven power demand; no P/E but momentum is improving.

previous_picks.rationale: Up +237% YTD, +40.22% in the last month, P/E of 34.14 is reasonable, news sentiment is strong at +0.408, and the 1-day gain of +1.96% shows momentum resilience during the recent broad selloff.
Up +645% over 1 year and +125.9% YTD with +14.30% in the last month, strong buy analyst consensus, positive news sentiment at +0.15, while the 1D move of +1.66% shows continued momentum.
1-year return of +594%, YTD +135.05% and a 1-month gain of +7.08% show robust momentum, backed by a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.29) despite negative news sentiment.

YTD return of +82.36% and a 1-month gain of +10.58% with positive news sentiment (+0.226) and strong_buy consensus; short interest is high at 25.59% which can drive sharp rallies on positive catalysts.
YTD surge of +714.98% and a 1-month return of +41.33% despite a high P/E of 91.95; the 1D move of +0.42% avoided the worst of Tuesday's memory selloff, supported by positive news sentiment of 0.164.

Up +852% over 1 year and +233.59% YTD, with a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.49) and a reasonable P/E of 49.75; the -14.52% 1D drop is a sharp pullback but driven by Korea leveraged ETF contagion, not fundamentals, and Dan Ives calls it a 'gut check moment' with Micron earnings the next catalyst ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/23/why-micron-stock-suddenly-crashed/), [benzinga.com](https://www.benzinga.com/markets/equities/26/06/60062350/dan-ives-says-ai-revolution-is-in-the-third-inning-calls-chip-sell-off-a-gut-check-moment)).

Up +257.62% YTD and +27.88% in the last month with a buy analyst consensus and neutral news sentiment; the -8.53% 1D drop follows sector-wide Korea-led selling but the 1W gain of +2.63% shows relative strength.

YTD of +613.41% and 1M of +23.53% with a buy analyst consensus; the -13.31% 1D crash on Korean contagion is severe, but the underlying Q3 beat (revenue $5.95B vs $4.73B consensus) and 645% datacenter YoY growth suggest fundamentals remain intact ([247wallst.com](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/23/sandisk-plunges-11-micron-and-western-digital-slide-10-as-korean-market-crash-hits-memory-chips/)).

YTD return of +261.83% and a 1-month gain of +22.80% with strong news sentiment (+0.141) and a buy consensus; the -5.04% 1D dip is modest relative to peers, and the 1W gain of +1.94% shows relative resilience.

1-year return of +305% and YTD +212.54% with a strong_buy consensus (score 1.45); the -8.08% 1D drop reflects broad AI spending fears, but the 1M gain of +33.99% underscores strong underlying demand for data infrastructure.
